Rate Limiter Module — Feature Description

The rate limiter module provides a sliding-window algorithm that restricts
the number of tool calls any single agent session can make within a given
time interval. It operates on a per-user, per-tool-name basis, using an
in-memory HashMap backed by an optional Redis store for cross-process
coordination.

Key behaviors:
- Default window: 60 seconds
- Default limit: 100 calls per window
- Burst allowance: up to 10 calls above the limit for short durations
- Configurable via RATE_LIMIT_WINDOW_SECS and RATE_LIMIT_MAX_CALLS env vars

The module exposes a single async function: check_and_record(user_id, tool_name).
It returns Ok(()) if the call is within quota or Err(RateLimitExceeded) if not.
Callers must not cache the result; every call must pass through the limiter
to keep the sliding window accurate.
